parsed_step.reasoning: The investigation focused on evaluating the trade-offs between stable low-diversity features and variable class-conditioned features within the backward packet length and bulk-related feature sets. Shortcut verification on a variable feature (Bwd Packet Length Max) revealed moderate shortcut signal, indicating some predictive leverage from class-conditioned variation. Conversely, a low-diversity bulk feature (Bwd Avg Bulk Rate) showed only weak shortcut evidence, and structural summary of a related bulk feature (Bwd Avg Bytes/Bulk) confirmed low variance and diversity, reinforcing its stability but limited discriminative power. These findings confirm the tension between stable near-constant features and variable features with structural class-conditioned variation, highlighting a trade-off in model reliance. Local contradictions related to duplicated rows and feature redundancy remain unresolved but acknowledged. Given the bounded local evidence and the exhausted budget, the task is concluded as completed with documented structural implications for interpretability.
